item.title: What I Shipped This Month Building Invoiso (v3.4.2)
item.description: One of the best things about working on Invoiso, a free offline invoice and billing tool available for Windows and Linux operating systems, has been “building in public.” This month has been particularly fruitful as a mix of long-pending features, quality-of-life enhancements, and critical bug fixes were included. All Changes in v3.4.2 👇 🔗 GitHub · Website · Download 💡 The Focus This Month This release is all about:

item.title: I Built a Free, Offline Invoice App with Flutter
item.description: Every time I needed to invoice a client, I ran into the same wall: every decent invoice tool either locks your data in the cloud, wants a monthly subscription, or both. I just wanted something that works offline, keeps my data on my machine, and doesn’t cost me anything. So I built it. Invoiso is a free, open-source desktop invoice app for Windows and Linux. No account. No internet. No recurring fee.

item.title: Understanding Stateless and Stateful Widgets in Flutter
item.description: Flutter, Google’s UI toolkit, enables developers to build natively compiled applications for mobile, web, and desktop from a single codebase. At the heart of Flutter’s UI framework are widgets, which form the building blocks of any application. In this article, we will explore two fundamental types of widgets in Flutter: Stateless Widgets Stateful Widgets. Watch on YouTube What Are Widgets in Flutter?

item.title: Build Your First Mobile-App in Flutter
item.description: What is Flutter? Flutter is a UI toolkit that is open-source and created by Google, enabling developers to build natively compiled applications for various platforms using a single codebase. It supports building apps for: Mobile: Android and iOS Web: Modern browsers Desktop: Windows, macOS, and Linux Embedded Devices Watch on YouTube Key Features of Flutter Single Codebase: Write one codebase for all platforms.
